cases.name: baseline
steps: assemble a multipart body with the two named text parts and one named file part under a single boundary
POST the multipart body to the target upload endpoint
assert the create status class
assert each text field is stored verbatim
capture the documented returned_file_url field
compute the stored file md5 and assert it equals the source md5 round-trip
re-fetch via the readback path and assert the persisted readback matches

cases.name: multi_file
steps: assemble two file parts sharing the one file field name, forming an array
POST the multipart body to the target upload endpoint
assert the create status class
assert exactly two stored entries under that field forming an array
assert each stored file md5 matches its source

cases.name: part_without_filename
steps: assemble a part with a Content-Disposition name but no filename
POST the multipart body to the target upload endpoint
assert it is parsed per the documented filename-absent rule
assert the resource reflects that classification

cases.name: duplicate_text_field
steps: assemble two text parts sharing one field name with distinct values
POST the multipart body to the target upload endpoint
assert the stored value matches the runtime-declared duplicate_policy exactly (first-wins, last-wins, or array)

cases.name: field_order_independence
steps: assemble the body with the file part first, then the two text parts
POST the multipart body to the target upload endpoint
assert the create status class
assert every text field stored verbatim and the file md5 matches, proving order independence

cases.name: malformed_boundary
steps: assemble a body whose declared Content-Type boundary token disagrees with the in-body part delimiters
POST the malformed multipart body to the target upload endpoint
assert 400/422
assert no resource was created
